title: New Boss for a Big Radio Show
summary: Rebecca Keating is the new editor of the Radio 4 Today programme, a popular news show on the BBC. She has worked at the BBC for almost 20 years on other important news programs. Rebecca says she is excited to lead the show and thinks it is very important because it helps people understand what is true and holds leaders accountable. The Today programme has over 5 million listeners. Rebecca will work with a team to make the show even better. She is taking over from Owenna Griffiths, who was the editor for more than five years. This change is part of keeping the show fresh and relevant for listeners.

keywords.explanation: the person in charge of a show or program
a famous news show on BBC radio
a big British company that makes TV and radio shows
people who tune in to hear the radio show
a group of people working together on the show

options: Lead it and help it thrive
questions.correct_answer: Lead it and help it thrive

background_read: The BBC is a big broadcasting company in the UK that makes news, entertainment, and educational shows. Radio 4 is one of its radio stations, and the Today programme is its most important morning news show, where people listen to updates and interviews. Editors are leaders who decide what stories to cover and how to present them to keep the show interesting and trustworthy for listeners.
Article_Structure: WHO: Rebecca Keating, a BBC employee with 20 years of experience.
WHAT: She becomes the new editor of the Radio 4 Today programme.
WHERE: At the BBC, specifically for Radio 4 in the UK.
WHY: To lead the show and help it stay important in sharing true news and holding leaders accountable.

perspectives.description: She feels privileged and wants to make the show thrive in a world where truth is hard to find.
